clusters.title: Spanish woman dies by euthanasia in case that drew national spotlight
clusters.neutral_headline: Spanish woman who died through euthanasia failed by state, say critics
bullet_summary: Reported by France24, BBC World, Guardian World

items.title: Spanish woman dies by euthanasia in case that drew national spotlight
items.standfirst: A 25-year-old woman underwent legal euthanasia in Spain on Thursday after a nearly two-year protracted legal battle and struggling with psychiatric illness since she was a teenager. Noelia Castillo's case was closely followed in Spain, which passed legislation in 2021 enshrining the right to euthanasia and medically assisted suicide for patients meeting certain conditions.
items.content: A 25-year-old woman underwent legal euthanasia in Spain on Thursday after a nearly two-year protracted legal battle and struggling with psychiatric illness since she was a teenager. Noelia Castillo's case was closely followed in Spain, which passed legislation in 2021 enshrining the right to euthanasia and medically assisted suicide for patients meeting certain conditions.

items.title: Spanish woman who died through euthanasia failed by state, say critics
items.standfirst: Noelia Castillo died on Thursday evening in a Barcelona hospital, after a protracted legal battle with her own father.
items.content: Noelia Castillo died on Thursday evening in a Barcelona hospital, after a protracted legal battle with her own father.

items.title: Spanish woman who won legal battle for right to euthanasia has assisted death
items.standfirst: Noelia Castillo, 25, a paraplegic, had suffered from psychiatric illness and lived in constant painA Spanish woman who spent months fighting her father for the right to euthanasia after being sexually assaulted and becoming paraplegic has finally ended her life on her own terms by means of an assisted death.Noelia Castillo, 25, had struggled with psychiatric illness since she was a teenager and tried to kill herself in October 2022 after being sexually assaulted. The attempt left her in constant pain and using a wheelchair. Eighteen months later, she used Spain’s euthanasia law, which was introduced in 2021, to secure permission to end her life.In Spain, Samaritans can be contacted on freephone 900 525 100. In the UK and Ireland, Samaritans can be contacted on freephone 116 123, or email jo@samaritans.org or jo@samaritans.ie Continue reading...
items.content: Noelia Castillo, 25, a paraplegic, had suffered from psychiatric illness and lived in constant painA Spanish woman who spent months fighting her father for the right to euthanasia after being sexually assaulted and becoming paraplegic has finally ended her life on her own terms by means of an assisted death.Noelia Castillo, 25, had struggled with psychiatric illness since she was a teenager and tried to kill herself in October 2022 after being sexually assaulted. The attempt left her in constant pain and using a wheelchair. Eighteen months later, she used Spain’s euthanasia law, which was introduced in 2021, to secure permission to end her life.In Spain, Samaritans can be contacted on freephone 900 525 100. In the UK and Ireland, Samaritans can be contacted on freephone 116 123, or email jo@samaritans.org or jo@samaritans.ie Continue reading...

clusters.title: DHS funding bill passes House, but shutdown drags on as Senate approves its own plan
clusters.neutral_headline: Why did House reject DHS bill? #politics
bullet_summary: The Department of Homeland Security said TSA employees could begin receiving paychecks again as early as Monday, after President Trump signed an executive order to restore their pay
House Speaker Mike Johnson said the lower chamber will vote Friday on its own plan to end the partial government shutdown, rather than the Senate-approved bill
The Department of Homeland Security said TSA agents should begin receiving pay as early as Monday, March 30

items.title: DHS funding bill passes House, but shutdown drags on as Senate approves its own plan
items.standfirst: The House passed a measure to fund the Department of Homeland Security for 60 days — but it's still unclear how the shutdown will end as the Senate, which approved its own funding plan, is on recess.
items.content: The House passed a measure to fund the Department of Homeland Security for 60 days — but it's still unclear how the shutdown will end as the Senate, which approved its own funding plan, is on recess.

items.title: Shutdown fight escalates as Johnson calls bipartisan Senate deal "detestable"
items.standfirst: House Speaker Mike Johnson said the lower chamber would vote "as soon as possible" on its own plan to fund the Department of Homeland Security after the Senate-approved legislation to fund most of the department, including the TSA, faced major hurdles in the House. Nikole Killion has the latest.

clusters.title: Three killed as tourist helicopter crashes on Hawaiian island of Kauai
clusters.neutral_headline: Three killed as tourist helicopter crashes on Hawaiian island of Kauai
bullet_summary: The helicopter was carrying one pilot and four passengers when it crashed on Thursday afternoon at Kalalau Beach, the Kauai fire department said
Reported by Guardian World, BBC World

items.title: Three killed as tourist helicopter crashes on Hawaiian island of Kauai
items.standfirst: Two others injured after sightseeing aircraft comes down on remote beach on Na Pali CoastA tourist helicopter crashed on a remote beach off the coast of Kauai, Hawaii, killing three people and injuring two others, authorities said.The helicopter was carrying one pilot and four passengers when it crashed on Thursday afternoon at Kalalau Beach, the Kauai fire department said. The beach is on the Na Pali coast on Kauai’s north shore. The area is otherwise reachable only by hiking or boat. Continue reading...
items.content: Two others injured after sightseeing aircraft comes down on remote beach on Na Pali CoastA tourist helicopter crashed on a remote beach off the coast of Kauai, Hawaii, killing three people and injuring two others, authorities said.The helicopter was carrying one pilot and four passengers when it crashed on Thursday afternoon at Kalalau Beach, the Kauai fire department said. The beach is on the Na Pali coast on Kauai’s north shore. The area is otherwise reachable only by hiking or boat. Continue reading...

items.title: House panel finds Florida Democrat guilty of ethics violations
items.standfirst: The House Ethics Committee has found evidence that Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick violated House rules. This comes after the panel held a rare public hearing to review investigations into allegations against the Florida Democrat.
items.content: The House Ethics Committee has found evidence that Rep. Sheila Cherfilus-McCormick violated House rules. This comes after the panel held a rare public hearing to review investigations into allegations against the Florida Democrat.(Image credit: Tom Williams/CQ-Roll Call, Inc via Getty Images)

clusters.title: Whale swims for freedom after big German rescue effort on Baltic coast
clusters.neutral_headline: Whale swims for freedom after big German rescue effort on Baltic coast
bullet_summary: Reported by BBC World, Sky News World

items.title: Whale swims for freedom after big German rescue effort on Baltic coast
items.standfirst: Rescue teams now believe the whale has reached deeper water in Lübeck Bay and hope it will head for the wider sea.
items.content: Rescue teams now believe the whale has reached deeper water in Lübeck Bay and hope it will head for the wider sea.

items.title: Whale freed by rescuers after days stranded on Baltic coast
items.standfirst: A humpback whale that was stranded in shallow water off the north German coast has been set free by rescuers.
items.content: A humpback whale that was stranded in shallow water off the north German coast has been set free by rescuers.
